title: Open problem: p-entropic matroids linear for prime p
statement_text: Our results leave open the possibility for $p$-entropic matroids to be linear whenever $p$ is prime.
context_text: The paper proves that $p$-entropic matroids are $\mathbb{F}_p$-representable for $p=2$ and $p=3$, and shows they are not linear for $p=9$ (a composite). The central remaining open problem is whether $p$-entropic matroids are always $\mathbb{F}_p$-representable when $p$ is prime, with only partial cases resolved in this work.
notes: PDF source — statement appears in the abstract as a description of what remains open; no labeled conjecture/problem environment visible in the extracted text. Full paper text is truncated, so additional explicit conjecture environments later in the paper may not have been captured.
